concerns.text: Task T038 description is corrupted with unrelated text: '...A duration of approximately half an hour will be allocated... on a -core runner'. The actual requirement (CPU-only execution, <30min runtime) is obscured, making the task unexecutable.

Task T040 instructs to 're-run primary t-tests INCLUDING participants who failed the manipulation check'. This contradicts spec FR-007 and Edge Cases which mandate exclusion from primary analysis. The task description is logically incoherent with the spec, preventing deterministic execution.

Task T036 describes calculating sample size for '[deferred] power' and comparing against actual N. The spec (FR-009, SC-002) requires a *prospective* power analysis to enforce N=300 *before* recruitment. The task description implies a post-hoc check only, missing the critical 'enforcement' deliverable.

Task T013 requires a 'rejection/regeneration loop' for sentiment differences but lacks a fallback strategy (e.g., max retries, manual override) if natural language constraints cannot be met. This risks an infinite loop, making the task non-deterministic without additional logic.

Task T016 logs randomization metadata to a JSON file. The spec (US-1) requires assignment to be recorded *before* survey display to prevent drift. The task description implies a post-hoc export, lacking the specific deliverable of 'real-time persistence' or 'pre-survey write'.

Task T037 specifies outputting `analysis_report.json`. The spec (US-3) and Constitution (Single Source of Truth) imply a need for a human-readable/paper-ready report. JSON alone may not satisfy the 'report' deliverable without a transformation task to generate a markdown/latex version.
